event_description: An airborne camera orbits a seaplane dock. Several seaplanes are moored to the floating docks. The camera tracks a specific red and white Harbour Air seaplane. In the EO view, all aircraft appear stationary and identical in status. The IR view reveals a distinct, bright thermal hotspot on the nose (engine cowling) of the tracked red and white seaplane, indicating recent or current engine operation, while the other docked aircraft appear cold-soaked.

qa.question: At the first close EO view, which nearby layout is visible around the red-and-white aircraft?

options.A: partial pale floatplane at the lower-left edge; dock structure along the right/lower side
options.B: partial pale floatplane at the lower-right edge; dock structure along the left/lower side
options.C: partial pale floatplane at the upper-left edge; dock structure along the right/lower side
options.D: partial pale floatplane at the upper-right edge; dock structure along the left/upper side

qa.evidence_note: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks

qa.question: In the high EO orbit, which dock-row placement is visible for the red aircraft?

options.A: red aircraft at the left end of the row; pale aircraft clustered to its right
options.B: red aircraft at the upper end of the row; pale aircraft clustered below it
options.C: red aircraft at the right end of the row; pale aircraft clustered to its left
options.D: red aircraft at the lower end of the row; pale aircraft clustered above it

qa.evidence_note: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks

qa.question: In the matching IR wide view, where is the compact aircraft return relative to the row?

options.A: left end of the aircraft row; cooler row shapes to its right
options.B: lower end of the aircraft row; cooler row shapes above it
options.C: right end of the aircraft row; cooler row shapes to its left
options.D: upper end of the aircraft row; cooler row shapes below it

qa.evidence_note: a nose hotspot marks the tracked seaplane while other aircraft are cold-soaked

qa.question: Around 00:32 EO, which dock/water relation sits around the red aircraft?

options.A: dock walkway right of the red aircraft; dark water left of it
options.B: dock walkway left of the red aircraft; dark water right of it
options.C: dock walkway above the red aircraft; dark water below it
options.D: dock walkway below the red aircraft; dark water above it

qa.evidence_note: transferring the hotspot to a neighboring docked aircraft

qa.question: Around 00:40, which EO/IR dock-side match is visible?

options.A: EO: dock/walkway above-right of the red aircraft; IR: dock/walkway return above-right of the aircraft return
options.B: EO: dock/walkway above-left of the red aircraft; IR: dock/walkway return above-left of the aircraft return
options.C: EO: dock/walkway below-right of the red aircraft; IR: dock/walkway return below-right of the aircraft return
options.D: EO: dock/walkway below-left of the red aircraft; IR: dock/walkway return below-left of the aircraft return

qa.evidence_note: EO: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks; IR: a nose hotspot marks the tracked seaplane while other aircraft are cold-soaked

qa.question: In the late close EO pass, which background placement stays with the aircraft?

options.A: dock edge above the aircraft; open water below it
options.B: dock edge right of the aircraft; open water left of it
options.C: dock edge below the aircraft; open water above it
options.D: dock edge left of the aircraft; open water right of it

qa.evidence_note: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks

qa.question: In the late close EO/IR interval, which background-side match is visible?

options.A: EO: dock above and water below; IR: bright dock band above and darker water texture below
options.B: EO: dock right and water left; IR: bright dock band right and darker water texture left
options.C: EO: dock below and water above; IR: bright dock band below and darker water texture above
options.D: EO: dock left and water right; IR: bright dock band left and darker water texture right

qa.evidence_note: EO: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks; IR: a nose hotspot marks the tracked seaplane while other aircraft are cold-soaked

qa.question: Which competing cue appears near the cross-modal match?

options.A: shoreline texture; thin EO structure near a warm region
options.B: water glare; bright EO patch beside a compact hotspot
options.C: adjacent cold aircraft; similar EO outline near the hotspot-bearing aircraft
options.D: dock hardware; straight-line EO cue near a compact hotspot

qa.evidence_note: transferring the hotspot to a neighboring docked aircraft

qa.question: After the view tightens, which EO background comparison is visible?

options.A: aircraft right of the dock-edge line; water beyond the right side of the aircraft
options.B: aircraft above the dock-edge line; water beyond the upper side of the aircraft
options.C: aircraft left of the dock-edge line; water beyond the left side of the aircraft
options.D: aircraft below the dock-edge line; water beyond the lower side of the aircraft

qa.evidence_note: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks

qa.question: Which IR background relation appears in the same close interval?

options.A: aircraft return just left of the bright dock band; water texture beyond the left side
options.B: aircraft return just right of the bright dock band; water texture beyond the right side
options.C: aircraft return just above the bright dock band; water texture beyond the upper side
options.D: aircraft return just below the bright dock band; water texture beyond the lower side

qa.evidence_note: a nose hotspot marks the tracked seaplane while other aircraft are cold-soaked

qa.question: Which combined EO/IR background sequence stays aligned?

options.A: EO: dock below the aircraft and water above; IR: bright dock band below the return and water texture above
options.B: EO: dock above the aircraft and water below; IR: bright dock band above the return and water texture below
options.C: EO: dock right of the aircraft and water left; IR: bright dock band right of the return and water texture left
options.D: EO: dock left of the aircraft and water right; IR: bright dock band left of the return and water texture right

qa.evidence_note: EO: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks; IR: a nose hotspot marks the tracked seaplane while other aircraft are cold-soaked

qa.question: For the full clip, which two-stage position chain is visible?

options.A: wide view: red aircraft at lower row end; close view: aircraft above dock line with water beyond upper side
options.B: wide view: red aircraft at left row end; close view: aircraft left of dock line with water beyond left side
options.C: wide view: red aircraft at right row end; close view: aircraft right of dock line with water beyond right side
options.D: wide view: red aircraft at upper row end; close view: aircraft below dock line with water beyond lower side

qa.evidence_note: EO: several aircraft appear stationary and similar in status at the docks; IR: a nose hotspot marks the tracked seaplane while other aircraft are cold-soaked

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
